# Overview

Jinxed is an implementation of a subset of the Python [curses] library.
It provides pure Python implementations of terminfo functions such as [tigetstr()]
and [tparm()] as well as convenience methods for working with Windows terminals.

Jinxed was initially written to support [Blessed] on Windows, but will work on all platforms.

# Installation

```console
$ pip install jinxed
```

# Documentation

Jinxed documentation can be found on [Read the Docs](https://jinxed.readthedocs.io/en/stable/).

[blessed]: https://pypi.org/project/blessed
[curses]: https://docs.python.org/library/curses.html
[tigetstr()]: https://docs.python.org/library/curses.html#curses.tigetstr
[tparm()]: https://docs.python.org/library/curses.html#curses.tparm

modules.name: jinxed
documentation:
Jinxed Terminal Library

Jinxed is an implementation of a subset of the Python curses library for Windows

Other libraries implement the full curses stack. Jinxed is intended primarily for libraries
that need to access terminfo functions such as tigetstr() and tparm().
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2019 - 2024 Avram Lubkin, All Rights Reserved
This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

modules.name: jinxed.terminfo
documentation:
jinxed terminal info library

Most of this information came from the terminfo man pages, part of ncurses
More information on ncurses can be found at:
https://www.gnu.org/software/ncurses/ncurses.html

Boolean and numeric capabilities are listed here to support tigetnum() and tigetflag()

modules.name: jinxed.terminfo.xterm
documentation:
xterm terminal info

Since most of the Windows virtual processing schemes are based on xterm
This file is intended to be sourced and includes the man page descriptions

Most of this information came from the terminfo man pages, part of ncurses
More information on ncurses can be found at:
https://www.gnu.org/software/ncurses/ncurses.html

The values are as reported by infocmp on Fedora 30 with ncurses 6.1

modules.name: jinxed._keys
documentation:
Key code constants

Most of this information came from the terminfo man pages, part of ncurses
More information on ncurses can be found at:
https://www.gnu.org/software/ncurses/ncurses.html

documentation:
Used as an error handler for Windows calls
Gets last error if call is not successful

functions.name: jinxed.win32.get_csbi
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`

Returns:

: {py:class}`ConsoleScreenBufferInfo`: [CONSOLE_SCREEN_BUFFER_INFO] structure

Wrapper for [GetConsoleScreenBufferInfo]

If `filehandle` is {py:data}`None`, uses the filehandle of {py:data}`sys.__stdout__`.

functions.name: jinxed.win32.get_console_input_encoding
documentation:
Returns:

: int: Current console mode

Query for the console input code page and provide an encoding

If the code page can not be resolved to a Python encoding, {py:data}`None` is returned.

functions.name: jinxed.win32.get_console_mode
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`

Returns:

: int: Current console mode

Raises:

: OSError: Error calling Windows API

Wrapper for [GetConsoleMode]

functions.name: jinxed.win32.set_console_mode
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`
  mode(int): Desired console mode

Raises:

: OSError: Error calling Windows API

Wrapper for [SetConsoleMode]

functions.name: jinxed.win32.setcbreak
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`

Raises:

: OSError: Error calling Windows API

Convenience function which mimics {py:func}`tty.setcbreak` behavior

All console input options are disabled except `ENABLE_PROCESSED_INPUT`
and, if supported, `ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_INPUT`

functions.name: jinxed.win32.setraw
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`

Raises:

: OSError: Error calling Windows API

Convenience function which mimics {py:func}`tty.setraw` behavior

All console input options are disabled except, if supported, `ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_INPUT`

functions.name: jinxed.win32.enable_vt_mode
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`

Raises:

: OSError: Error calling Windows API

Enables virtual terminal processing mode for the given console

If `filehandle` is {py:data}`None`, uses the filehandle of {py:data}`sys.__stdout__`.

functions.name: jinxed.win32.get_terminal_size
documentation:
Args:

: fd(int): Python file descriptor

Returns:

: {py:class}`os.terminal_size`: Named tuple representing terminal size

Convenience function for getting terminal size

In Python 3.3 and above, this is a wrapper for {py:func}`os.get_terminal_size`.
In older versions of Python, this function calls [GetConsoleScreenBufferInfo].

functions.name: jinxed.win32.flush_and_set_console
documentation:
Args:

: filehandle(int): Windows filehandle object as returned by {py:func}`msvcrt.get_osfhandle`
  mode(int): Desired console mode. If None, mode is not changed

Attempts to set console to specified mode, but will not raise on failure

If the file descriptor is STDOUT or STDERR, attempts to flush first

functions.name: jinxed.win32.get_term
documentation:
Args:

: fd(int): Python file descriptor
  fallback(bool): Use fallback terminal type if type can not be determined

Returns:

: str: Terminal type

Attempts to determine and enable the current terminal type

The current logic is:

> - If TERM is defined in the environment, the value is returned
> - Else, if ANSICON is defined in the environment, `'ansicon'` is returned
> - Else, if virtual terminal mode is natively supported,
>   it is enabled and `'vtwin10'` is returned
> - Else, if `fallback` is `True`, Ansicon is loaded, and `'ansicon'` is returned
> - If no other conditions are satisfied, `'unknown'` is returned

This logic may change in the future as additional terminal types are added.

classes.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._literal_percent
documentation: Literal percent sign

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._pop_c
documentation: Return pop() like %c in printf

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._increment_one_two
documentation:
Add 1 to first two parameters
Missing parameters are treated as 0's

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._binary_op
documentation:
Perform a binary operation on the last two items on the stack
The order of evaluation is the order the items were placed on the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._unary_op
documentation: Perform a unary operation on the last item on the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._push_param
documentation:
Push a parameter onto the stack
If the parameter is missing, push Null

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._set_dynamic
documentation: Set the a dynamic variable to pop()

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._get_dynamic
documentation: Push the value of a dynamic variable onto the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._set_static
documentation: Set the a static variable to pop()

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._get_static
documentation: Push the value of a static variable onto the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._char_constant
documentation: Push an character constant onto the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._int_constant
documentation: Push an integer constant onto the stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._push_len
documentation: Replace the last item on the stack with its length

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._cond_if
documentation: Recursively evaluate the body of the if statement

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._cond_then_else
documentation:
If the last item on the stack is True,
recursively evaluate then statement

Do not consume last item on stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._cond_then_fi
documentation:
If the last item on the stack is True,
recursively evaluate then statement

Always consume last item on stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._cond_else
documentation:
If the last item on the stack is False,
recursively evaluate the both of the else statement

Always consume last item on stack

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._cond_fi
documentation: End if statement

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._printf
documentation: Subset of printf-like formatting

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._unmatched
documentation: Escape pattern with no spec is skipped

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm._literal
documentation: Anything not prefaced with a known pattern spec is treated literally

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm.parse
documentation:
Parsing loop
Evaluate regex patterns in order until a pattern is matched

methods.name: jinxed._tparm.TParm.child
documentation: Return a new instance with the same variables, but different parameters
Class to hold tparm methods and persist variables between calls
